reasoning: The baseline price is $99.93 (the market close on Friday, April 25, which applies to Sunday, April 27). The stock has been in a slow downtrend over the past several months, resulting in a linear trend model estimate of approximately 0.47 for the price to be higher by early May. Short-term price movement will likely be dominated by the Q1 earnings report scheduled for April 29. Heading into earnings, analysts have actively lowered their price targets (e.g., Citi cutting to $113 in early April) due to expected weakness, with consensus Q1 EPS projected down 12.9% year-over-year. While this lowered bar could lead to an earnings beat, the prevailing momentum remains tilted slightly negative. Thus, I estimate a 47% chance for the May 4 resolution. Over longer horizons, as the immediate effects of Q1 earnings and current downward momentum dissipate, standard equity valuation fundamentals—which suggest a slight upward drift over long periods—contend with increasing general uncertainty. Adhering to the principle that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon, the probabilities gradually revert to the maximum entropy state of 0.50 for the most distant dates.
